Core Spec-Driven Development Pipeline

Gate-to-SHA freshness binding (commit records, sha reads, verify-gate hard-blocks on stale)
Council governance lifecycle (review -> triage -> human gate, threaded by suggestions.md)
Council Review Round (deck to gate)
Agent Assembly at Dispatch (D40)
Gate-Capable Autonomy System (D9)
Three-stage council review pipeline (deck-prep to chairman synthesis)
Council extension governed pipeline (review, triage, human gate)
Council gate to git-ext gate-SHA binding and stale hard-block
Graph-grounded SDD loop (graph → context → tasks → parallel implement)
Optional grounding-hook wiring (extension → hooks → command → skill)
000-sample conformance-fixture artifact tree

Three-stage council review roles (opinions → peer review → chairman synthesis)
Gate-SHA approval-integrity mechanism
Council end-to-end loop (deck → review → triage → human gate, SC-001)
Five-lens council panel (correctness/risk/simplicity/testability/sequencing)
Convergent optional:true / prose-only hard-block defect (round headline)
Workforce pair pipeline: categorize → assemble → workforce gate

nodes.label: SpecSeyal SDD Orchestrator
nodes.rationale: Extended spec-driven-development workflow on GitHub Spec Kit + graphify: a governed pipeline from spec to council-defended plan to specialized parallel implementation to testing, with an Agent SDK-native platform layer to come. Dogfoods itself from M1 onward.

nodes.label: Principle: Artifacts Are the Contract
nodes.rationale: Every phase reads artifacts in and writes exactly one artifact out, so the CLI and the future platform share one interface and each phase stays idempotent/resumable from artifacts alone.

nodes.label: Principle: Context Hygiene
nodes.rationale: Main thread stays lean; heavy work runs in separate sessions that return compact artifacts. Enables long pipelines without context bloat.

nodes.label: Model Policy (D18)
nodes.rationale: Opus for orchestration and judgment roles (council chairman, analyze/triage); Sonnet for implementation and mechanical roles (deck prep, categorizer, council members); Haiku unused in v1 — matches model cost to cognitive load of the role.

nodes.label: Subscription-Only Billing (D28)
nodes.rationale: Never set or rely on ANTHROPIC_API_KEY; all work runs on the Claude subscription. M6+ programmatic sessions use the plan's Agent SDK credit. Avoids metered API billing.

nodes.label: Extension Hooks Registry (extensions.yml)
nodes.rationale: Registers before_/after_ phase hooks that wire the graphify (context grounding) and git (phase-tagged commit, gate verification, gate recording) extensions into the stock speckit phase commands. before_* gate hooks are hard-blocks; auto_execute_hooks is on.

nodes.label: Plan Defense Council Extension (README)
nodes.rationale: Forces every plan.md through an adversarial council (deck prep, independent opinions, anonymized peer review, chairman synthesis, triage, human gate) before task breakdown, so a plan survives contact with skepticism before /speckit-tasks runs. Command-invoked, not a pipeline hook.

nodes.label: Council Extension Config (council-config.yml)
nodes.rationale: Governs the council: member_count (5, the M1 cost lever), member_lenses (correctness/risk/simplicity/testability/sequencing), the D18 model map (opus chairman, sonnet member/deck-prep), max_rounds (1), and council_tier defaults.

nodes.label: Council Extension Manifest (extension.yml)
nodes.rationale: Declares the council extension (id, MIT license, requires speckit >=0.2.0) and the three provided commands; tagged council/plan-review/governance/llm-council.

nodes.label: Council Ceremony Tier (council_tier, D56)
nodes.rationale: full vs standard ceremony (D56, M3 cost review). full = per-member peer review + eager context + no output cap (the 002 5.25M baseline, 12 sessions). standard = consolidated single peer critique + lazy context + output cap (8 sessions), cutting cache-creation churn. Absent resolves to full (safest/most-thorough), never a silent downgrade.

nodes.label: Council Human Gate command (/speckit-council-approve)
nodes.rationale: Runs no session and writes no trace — a human acts; records the ## Human Gate decision (approved / approved-with-notes / rejected) that unlocks /speckit-tasks, rejection returns the plan for one revision round. Under gates.council.mode:auto (only within full_auto) triage writes the gate and this command is verify-only; disable-model-invocation:true — never model-invoked.

nodes.label: Council Review command (/speckit-council)
nodes.rationale: Dispatches 1 deck-prep (Sonnet) -> 5 members x 2 stages (Sonnet, parallel) -> 1 chairman (Opus); only the chairman's suggestions.md crosses back to the main thread (context hygiene), member opinions stay chairman-only. Degrades gracefully with a reduced-grounding flag when no graph exists (FR-019); --reopen delta|full re-runs on a reopened plan (D14).

nodes.label: Council Triage command (/speckit-council-triage)
nodes.rationale: Reads round-N/suggestions.md only (never opinions/ — the context-hygiene boundary); records every suggestion's disposition with reasoning (D13.5). Convergence (D13): zero blocking -> gate; >=1 blocking -> one revision + chairman-only delta check -> gate; a residual blocking escalates to the human.

nodes.label: Chairman Prompt Template (synthesis & delta-check)
nodes.rationale: A prompt template rendered with {{slots}} and handed to the Opus xhigh chairman subagent as the literal prompt argument. Two modes: synthesis (stage 3 — reads all opinions/ + peer/, consolidates/classifies/IDs, writes suggestions.md from scratch) and delta-check (re-adjudicates only prior blocking IDs against the revised plan, mints no new IDs, never re-opens opinions/ — that omission is what keeps the delta check cheap, FR-010).

nodes.label: Defense Deck Overview Template (one-page, non-technical)
nodes.rationale: One-page non-technical rendering for the human gate; a reviewer must reach an approve/reject decision from this page alone without opening technical.md (SC-007, principle 8). Git-versioned in place, overwritten on every revision (D38), not round-scoped.

nodes.label: Defense Deck Technical Template
nodes.rationale: The council members' and chairman's primary document, read in full (FR-005): problem restatement, chosen approach + rejected alternatives, dependency/graph impact, risk register, cost estimate, testability claim. Git-versioned in place and overwritten on revision (D38). A plan with no rejected alternatives reads as unexamined and is a suggestion magnet.

nodes.label: Council Member Base Reviewer Prompt
nodes.rationale: The thin member interface (FR-003) /speckit-council injects into each member subagent for both stages; a member is anything that receives (deck, plan, tools) and returns a review file, so swapping the general bench for a role-critic roster is a rewrite of this file only. Members are general-purpose reviewers with a soft lens nudge, return status only (S2), and write all content to opinions/.

nodes.label: Suggestions Template (round-N/suggestions.md)
nodes.rationale: The compression boundary — the only file under council/ the main thread reads and the sole input to triage (FR-007, principle 1); round-scoped and never overwritten (contrast defense-deck/, overwritten in place, D38). The Verdict counts drive convergence: zero blocking routes straight to the human gate, >=1 blocking forces one revision + chairman-only delta check (FR-010).

nodes.label: Council Trace Fragment Spec
nodes.rationale: Per council round /speckit-council dispatches 12 sessions (1 deck-prep, 10 member, 1 chairman) + triage's 1 = 13 total, each emitting exactly one trace; the orchestrator appends fragments serially at each stage barrier (never parallel — a concurrent write would corrupt the JSON Lines file, research.md R-D3) and stamps parent_trace_id. Fixed council values: agent_id/skills/elevated_grants empty (non-implementer), cost_usd null (D28), schema_version 1.0.

nodes.label: Per-Feature Git Lifecycle Extension README
nodes.rationale: Describes the git extension payload: four primitives (commit/sha/verify-gate/cleanup), the after_* hook table, and the mechanical-git-zero-AI stance (no model calls, no traces.jsonl records, FR-007/D25). Only cleanup is human-facing; commit/sha/verify-gate are called by hooks and by council-approve / implement-parallel at the two boundaries with no stock hook slot.

nodes.label: Git Extension Manifest (extension.yml)
nodes.author: narenkarthikbm
nodes.rationale: Declares the four primitives and the hook table (all optional:false = auto-invoked/hard-block, vs graphify's optional:true announce-only). v1 enforcement is prose-level — the invoking phase-skill honors the hook exit code; a code-enforced HookExecutor is deferred to M6 (D53). The installer inserts git's verify-gate AHEAD of graphify's context hook on before_tasks/before_implement (R1-S07).

nodes.label: Git Config (git-config.yml)
nodes.rationale: Mechanical-git policy: base_branch main, commit-message grammar (FR-006), branch-name pattern (incl. timestamp mode), merge policy ff-permitted with merge --no-ff only if base diverged (D52), mandatory annotated anchor tag complete/<spec-id>, and the gate->(artifacts, verify-before) map. Nothing here invokes a model.

nodes.label: Git Cleanup command (/speckit-git-cleanup)
nodes.rationale: The one human-facing git primitive; integrates the feature branch into base_branch (ff permitted, merge --no-ff only if base diverged, D52), tags the mandatory annotated anchor complete/<spec-id>, deletes the branch. On conflict it aborts (git merge --abort) and surfaces — never auto-resolves, never deletes an unmerged branch. Idempotent; no trace. Shells out to scripts/cleanup.sh.

nodes.label: Git Commit command (/speckit-git-commit)
nodes.rationale: The primitive behind every after_* hook and implement-parallel's per-wave commits; stages the feature's changed paths and commits with the FR-006 phase-tagged grammar, no-op (exit 0) if the tree is clean (FR-004). Prints the new SHA. Mechanical git only — no model, no trace. Runs scripts/commit.sh.

nodes.label: Git Sha command (/speckit-git-sha)
nodes.rationale: Read-only: prints the SHA of the HEAD commit that last touched an artifact path — the value source a gate section (## Human Gate / ## Workforce Gate) records against (FR-008). No side effect, no trace. Runs scripts/sha.sh.

nodes.label: Git Verify-Gate command (/speckit-git-verify-gate)
nodes.rationale: Parses the recorded <artifact> @ <sha> from the gate section and compares each against the artifact's current SHA; working-tree-aware and fail-closed — a non-zero exit hard-blocks the phase (FR-009). Runs from before_tasks (council gate) and before_implement (workforce gate). Read-only, no trace. Runs scripts/verify-gate.sh.

nodes.label: Graphify Grounding Extension README
nodes.rationale: Grounds Spec Kit in the repo's graphify knowledge graph so planning/tasks/implement reflect what the code actually is, not what the model guesses. Provides the context command and three optional:true hooks (before_plan/tasks/implement). Companion siblings /speckit-tasks-graph and /speckit-implement-parallel are separate skills, not edits to stock commands, so a Spec Kit re-init does not clobber them.

nodes.label: Graphify Extension Manifest (extension.yml)
nodes.author: narenkarthikbm
nodes.rationale: Declares the speckit.graphify.context command and three hooks, all optional:true — they surface as a suggested command rather than auto-running, unlike git's optional:false hard-block hooks (this optional:true announce-only default was the round-1 headline defect that git's manifest contrasts against).

nodes.label: Graphify Config (graphify-config.yml)
nodes.rationale: Graph paths (repo graphify-out/graph.json, merged cross-repo ../graphify-out/graph.json), the output file graphify-context.md written into the feature dir, and a per-query token budget of 1200 to keep the context file lean.

nodes.label: Graphify Context command (/speckit-graphify-context)
nodes.rationale: Queries graphify query/explain/path against the repo (or merged stack) graph, capped by the configured token budget, and writes a lean citation-backed graphify-context.md (relevant modules, per-file blast radius, shared/mutable files, patterns). If no graph.json exists it reports that /graphify must be run first and writes nothing. LLM-driven graph querying, no standalone script.

nodes.label: SpecSeyal Constitution
nodes.rationale: The operative gate that /speckit-plan and /speckit-analyze check each feature against; supersedes convenience. Every /speckit-plan runs a Constitution Check before Phase 0 and re-checks after design; an unjustified violation is an ERROR, a justified one is recorded in Complexity Tracking with the rejected simpler alternative. Amending it is a decision (D-row in docs/90) plus a version bump. v1.0.0, ratified 2026-07-09.

nodes.label: Spec Kit Checklist Template
nodes.rationale: Stock Spec Kit template the /speckit-checklist command fills from feature context (spec.md requirements, plan.md technical context, tasks.md details); the sample CHK001-CHK006 items must be replaced, not kept.

nodes.label: Principle I — Artifacts Are the Contract
nodes.source_location: Core Principles / I. Artifacts Are the Contract
nodes.rationale: Every phase reads artifacts in and writes exactly one out (principle 1, D4) so CLI and platform share one interface (files in the repo); a phase writing two artifacts or mutating another's violates this. tasks.md's disjoint ## Execution Waves append (graphify) is the sole two-writer exception — which is why categorization got its own artifact (D37).
nodes.norm_label: principle i — artifacts are the contract

nodes.label: Principle II — Context Hygiene
nodes.rationale: Main thread stays a lean orchestration spine; heavy work runs in separate sessions receiving only declared context-in and returning one compact artifact, so the main thread never inherits an offloaded session's context. The council's opinions/ never cross to the main thread — suggestions.md is the sole compression boundary (docs/10 6).

nodes.label: Principle III — Resumability (non-negotiable)
nodes.rationale: Phase state is inferred from artifacts alone — no state file, adding one is forbidden (D32). A phase is complete iff its artifact-out exists AND validates against its contract; gate decisions are artifacts (a section appended to a record), never DB or transcript events. Kill the process anywhere; the artifact tree alone says where to restart.

nodes.label: Principle IV — Observability
nodes.rationale: Every session in every phase appends exactly one trace record to traces.jsonl (principle 4, D35), no opt-out. Traces carry no message bodies (identity + metrics only) so they sync to a multi-tenant manager without redaction review. Tokens are the unit of account; cost_usd is null under subscription billing (D6, D28).

nodes.label: Principle V — Subscription-Only Billing (non-negotiable)
nodes.rationale: All work runs on Claude subscription auth; ANTHROPIC_API_KEY is never set or relied upon (D28) because a build machine with the key set silently routes billing to the API. M0-M5 run interactive; M6+ programmatic sessions use the plan's Agent SDK monthly credit.

nodes.label: Model Policy (D18) — two-plane Opus/Sonnet
nodes.rationale: Two-plane: Opus xhigh on the main thread and for judgment roles (council chairman, analyze/triage); Sonnet for implementation agents and mechanical/generative roles (deck-prep, categorizer, skill builder, council members). Haiku unused in v1. Only base specialists declare a model; a (role, model) trace contradiction is flagged, never silently rewritten — the trace is evidence, not an enforcer.

nodes.label: Autonomy & Gates (D9)
nodes.rationale: Exactly two gate-capable checkpoints: the council gate (post-plan) and the workforce gate (post-tasks + agent assignment). The council gate is default-on in every profile; skipping it requires an explicit full_auto:true handshake. Autonomy is about who signs, never about what runs — an auto gate still convenes the council and writes every artifact.

nodes.label: Three-Stage Council Review flow
nodes.rationale: Independent opinions -> anonymized peer review -> chairman synthesis (FR-004). Members form honest adversarial assessments alone in stage 1 before seeing peers in stage 2; only the chairman's consolidated suggestions.md crosses back to the main thread, member opinions stay chairman-only.

nodes.label: Council Anonymity invariant (FR-006)
nodes.rationale: A member is known only by its letter (A-E); the letter->identity mapping is never persisted anywhere — not in opinion files, not in suggestions.md's Sources column. The chairman must not reveal which member wrote which opinion, so suggestions are weighed on merit, not source, and no downstream reader can de-anonymize a reviewer.

nodes.label: Reduced-Grounding flag (FR-019)
nodes.rationale: When graphify-out/graph.json is absent a member proceeds deck-only rather than blocking (grounding enhances review quality, it is not a precondition) but must emit the exact 'Reduced grounding' banner so the chairman detects it and opens suggestions.md with the reduced-grounding line — a degraded review must never read as fully grounded (D46).

nodes.label: Council Ceremony Tier (D56) — full vs standard
nodes.rationale: Ceremony scales by council_tier: full = 5 per-member peer reviews (12 sessions) with eager context loading; standard = one consolidated peer critique (opinions/peer/consolidated.md, <=15 points) plus lazy on-demand context (technical deck is the only required read) for 8 sessions. Lazy loading is a cost lever, not a licence to review less carefully.
nodes.norm_label: council ceremony tier (d56) — full vs standard

nodes.label: Graphify grounding tool for council members (D10, FR-005)
nodes.rationale: Members call the installed graphify CLI (explain/query/path) directly against graphify-out/graph.json to check deck claims about blast radius/dependencies instead of trusting the deck — no new tool exists, the CLI is the tool. A graph result that contradicts a deck claim becomes a classified suggestion (D10 receipts-checking).
nodes.norm_label: graphify grounding tool for council members (d10, fr-005)

nodes.label: Gate-to-SHA binding (gates.yml)
nodes.rationale: A GateSHABinding (<artifact> @ <sha>) recorded in the git-ext-owned specs/NNN/gates.yml is fresh iff the recorded SHA equals the artifact's current commit SHA AND its working tree is clean (R1-S05); the gate section in the artifact carries only a one-line reference. verify-gate fails closed — blocks — on a stale, unparseable, or gate-format-version-drifted binding so a gated phase can't proceed on an approval that no longer matches the artifact (FR-008/009).

nodes.label: Council Gate (binds plan.md, verify before tasks)
nodes.rationale: Guards the tasks phase: binds plan.md; verify-gate runs at before_tasks and hard-blocks if the plan changed since council approval. The after_council_approve hook writes plan.md @ <sha> into gates.yml (signer-agnostic — must fire on an auto-written gate too).
nodes.norm_label: council gate (binds plan.md, verify before tasks)

nodes.label: Workforce Gate (binds tasks.md + assignment.md, verify before implement)
nodes.rationale: Guards the implement phase: binds tasks.md + assignment.md; verify-gate runs at before_implement and is re-checked every implement wave (R1-S23).
nodes.norm_label: workforce gate (binds tasks.md + assignment.md, verify before implement)

nodes.label: Principle: Artifacts Are the Contract
nodes.rationale: Every phase reads artifacts in, writes exactly one artifact out. Files in the repo are the sole interface between the CLI pipeline layer and the platform layer, so everything built CLI-first is reusable by the orchestrator with zero rework (D4).

nodes.label: Principle: Context Hygiene
nodes.rationale: The main thread stays a lean orchestration spine; heavy work runs in separate sessions returning only compact artifacts, so offloaded-session context never leaks back into the main thread.

nodes.label: Principle: Resumability
nodes.rationale: Every phase is idempotent and resumable from artifacts alone; kill the orchestrator mid-council and on restart it picks up where the artifacts say it stopped. Designed in from day one, never retrofitted.

nodes.label: Principle: Observability
nodes.rationale: Every session leaves a trace (role, model, tokens, duration). Cost is a tracked metric inside observability, deliberately not a governing principle (D6).

nodes.label: Session Boundary Rule
nodes.rationale: Each offloaded phase receives a minimal context package (the artifacts it needs) and returns exactly one artifact; the main thread never inherits an offloaded session's context.

nodes.label: Two-Plane Model Policy (D18)
nodes.rationale: Sonnet default for implementation agents; Opus (xhigh) for the main thread. Judgment roles (chairman, analyze/triage) take Opus; mechanical/generative roles (deck prep, categorizer, skill builder, members) take Sonnet; Haiku unused in v1. Graph-scored assignment deferred until observability produces real data.

nodes.label: Council Gate
nodes.rationale: Post-plan correctness gate, default-on in every profile; skipping it requires an explicit full-auto profile (D9). The human is always the backstop.

nodes.label: Workforce Gate
nodes.rationale: Post-tasks + agent-assignment economic gate before implementation spends tokens (D9). Its roster displays every agent's elevated-grant set — approving the roster is approving network access (D41).

nodes.label: Library Flywheel (D24)
nodes.rationale: Good generated skills get persisted back into the library with their stats. The only self-evolving component in year one (D24 year-one boundary).

nodes.label: Dogfooding Rule
nodes.rationale: From M1 onward the workflow builds itself: each milestone gets a spec and the council reviews the plan for building the next milestone. Free testing, and observability data accumulates immediately. M0 is the only plain-session milestone.

nodes.label: Subscription-Only Billing (D28)
nodes.rationale: M0-M5 run in interactive Claude Code sessions; M6+ programmatic sessions run on the Agent SDK monthly credit. ANTHROPIC_API_KEY stays unset on build machines so billing never silently routes to the API.

nodes.label: Defense Deck Preparation (Session A)
nodes.rationale: A separate session reads plan.md, spec.md and the graph summary and produces a technical + non-technical defense deck justifying the plan (chosen approach, rejected alternatives, risk register).

nodes.label: Chairman Synthesis (Stage 3)
nodes.rationale: One Opus chairman consolidates member opinions + peer reviews into suggestions.md and classifies each as blocking/strong/consider. The chairman synthesis is the compression boundary — the only thing that returns to the main thread (context hygiene).

nodes.label: Convergence Rule (one round, D13)
nodes.rationale: v1 runs exactly one full round; blocking items trigger one revision + a cheap chairman-only delta check before the human gate. Chairman classification is safe because the human gate is default-on (D9). Rejections must be logged with reasoning; silent dropping is forbidden.

nodes.label: Graphify Query Tool (receipts-checking, D10)
nodes.rationale: Council members hold an on-demand graphify query/explain/path tool so they can verify a claimed blast radius against the actual dependency graph instead of taking the deck's word — reviewers that can check receipts.

nodes.label: Reduced-Grounding Flag
nodes.rationale: When no graphify graph exists the council runs deck-only and surfaces an explicit reduced-grounding flag in suggestions.md and the decision record, visible at the human gate — never presented as fully grounded (D46 FR-019).

nodes.label: Agent Assembly / Composability (D40)
nodes.rationale: An agent is not stored; it is assembled at dispatch: base model + base specialist config (by type,specialization) + injected skill modules (by tags) + tool grants (union of skills). The library's unit of storage is the skill, not the agent. Deterministic assembly is what makes the workforce gate reviewable and implement resumable.

nodes.label: Base Specialist
nodes.rationale: A thin, hand-curated lane definition in .claude/agents/, selected by exactly one (type,specialization) lane. Curated-static: created/changed by a human under a D-row only, never by the flywheel (D44). Carries no tags — tags select skills, never bases.

nodes.label: Assembly Cap of 3 (D40)
nodes.rationale: At most base + 3 injected skills per dispatch. The assignment step trims by tag-rank and logs what it drops, never exceeding. Bounds the blast radius of contradictory skill pairs (S4).

nodes.label: Core Toolset (defined term, D44)
nodes.rationale: The fixed set {Read, Write, Edit, Bash, Glob, Grep} every base carries unmodified. Never listed in a grant set or a trace — it is assumed, so only elevation is auditable.

nodes.label: Elevated Grant (D41)
nodes.rationale: Any tool access beyond the core toolset (web search first among them). Declared by a skill, never an agent-level default; reaches a dispatch only by that skill being injected. Displayed on the workforce-gate roster and recorded on the trace — approving the roster is approving network access.

nodes.label: Skill Module (SKILL.md)
nodes.rationale: The library's unit of storage and the skill builder's output — a native Claude Code SKILL.md carrying SpecSeyal metadata, selected by tags, injected onto a base. Growing composable parts instead of 88 near-empty agent lanes (D40).

nodes.label: Additive-Only Rule (D40.4)
nodes.rationale: A skill MUST extend the base, never override/contradict/weaken it — it may forbid more, never permit more. Assembly is a union not an override chain, so three skills injected in any order produce the same agent; that determinism is what lets three be injected sight-unseen and keeps the workforce gate reviewable.

nodes.label: refactor-discipline Seed Skill
nodes.rationale: The first seed module and the reason OQ1 could be overruled: it carries the discipline that refactor-as-a-type existed to supply (bound blast radius, add no public surface), auto-injected whenever preserves_behavior is true, without costing a type.

nodes.label: Skill Builder (was gap generator, D2/D40)
nodes.rationale: On a tag with no covering skill it authors one SKILL.md module rather than a whole bespoke agent, so the library grows in composable parts. Its output is persisted by the flywheel.

nodes.label: Type Axis (8 values)
nodes.rationale: scaffold/data-model/service/endpoint/ui/test/docs/infra. Mechanical, verifiable, cheap — derivable from graphify's files=/deps=/mutates=/phase/TDD-position signals. No integration type: pure shared-manifest wiring is orchestrator glue, not a task.

nodes.label: Specialization Axis (11 values)
nodes.rationale: Exactly one per task — the expertise that dominates. Interpretive, needs judgment: derived from plan.md's stack + path conventions + spec domain, not from graphify. This derivability asymmetry vs type is what justifies two axes at all.

nodes.label: preserves_behavior Modifier (OQ1)
nodes.rationale: A boolean modifier orthogonal to type (refactor overruled as a type, D42), true when every file already exists and the task adds no public surface. When true it auto-injects the refactor-discipline skill — discipline by composition rather than by replacing the base with a generic agent.

nodes.label: general Specialization 20% Cap (OQ3)
nodes.rationale: general is the honest escape hatch when no lane dominates, but at most 20% of a feature's tasks may be general or categorization FAILS and must be redone. The cap is a floor on evidence: a feature whose tasks resist classification is telling you the plan is underspecified.

nodes.label: Free Tags (three jobs, D16/D40)
nodes.rationale: Lowercase kebab-case, unbounded. Do three jobs: rank candidate skills (Jaccard), brief the skill builder on empty match, and select the skills injected at assembly. Job 3 recovers the cross-cutting signal OQ2 gave up — it moved from the matching key to the assembly, never lost.

nodes.label: Autonomy Profile (profile.yaml)
nodes.rationale: Per-feature declaration of which of the two gate-capable checkpoints stop for a human (D8/D9). Absent file ⇒ both gates human — a missing file is the safest profile, never the fastest (P1).

nodes.label: Full-Auto Two-Key Handshake (D9/D33)
nodes.rationale: gates.council.mode:auto is invalid without full_auto:true, and full_auto:true is invalid unless both gates are auto. The field is redundant as data and load-bearing as ceremony: it makes the diff that disables adversarial review impossible to read as an accident.

nodes.label: council_tier Ceremony Knob (D56)
nodes.rationale: full (default, 12 sessions, eager context, per-member peer) vs standard (8 sessions, lazy context, one consolidated peer, capped output). A cost/thoroughness knob orthogonal to gate mode: mode says who signs, tier says how the council deliberates. Absent ⇒ full. Never changes the D18 model map.

nodes.label: traces.jsonl Observability Record
nodes.rationale: Append-only JSONL, one record per session, no message bodies (context hygiene + multi-tenant safe). Carries the assembly (skills[], elevated_grants) per D43; cost_usd is null under subscription billing — tokens are the unit of account.

nodes.label: capture_method (exact-or-null, D47)
nodes.rationale: sdk | transcript | unavailable. tokens may be null only when unavailable — never a fabricated estimate, the same ethos as cost_usd:null (D35). Spend rollups skip null-token records and MUST report their count so completeness is visible.

nodes.label: council_spend Rollup (M1 exit metric)
nodes.rationale: phase_spend(council)+phase_spend(deck-prep), computed on tokens_billable = input+output+cache_creation (cache_read excluded as saving not spend). This is the M1 exit criterion 'council token spend per feature is measured', reported with the count of unavailable records.

nodes.label: skill_success Flywheel Attribution (D43)
nodes.rationale: Keys on the skill id@version, not the dispatched agent, because crediting the base would smear each skill's record across every co-injected skill, and ignoring version would let a regressed prompt coast on an older one's numbers. A skill is credited only in combination with its assembly-mates — honest, sufficient for a promotion bar.

nodes.label: Decision Record (audit trail, D13.5)
nodes.rationale: Append-only council audit trail. Its single reason to exist: a suggestion may be rejected but may not be silently dropped — every non-acceptance carries written reasoning. A deferred with nowhere to resurface is a rejected wearing a nicer word.

nodes.label: Phase State Inferred from Artifacts (D32)
nodes.rationale: Phase state is inferred from artifacts; there is no state file and adding one is forbidden. A phase is complete iff its artifact-out exists and validates. Consequence: gate decisions are artifacts (sections appended to a file), not events in a database.

nodes.label: Artifact Ownership Rule (§6)
nodes.rationale: No writer touches another's artifact. tasks.md has two writers only because graphify appends a disjoint section; that hazard is why categorization was given its own artifact rather than another tasks.md section (D37).

nodes.label: Cross-Extension Seam Convention (§9, D57)
nodes.rationale: Cross-extension coupling attaches at a hook point, never a source edit to another extension's installer-overwritten file (every install.sh does rm -rf + cp -R and silently wipes foreign edits). The same ownership principle as §6, applied at the code layer instead of the artifact layer. Reinstall-survival is a test, not a hope.

nodes.label: Transcript-Based Token Capture
nodes.rationale: The interactive path (capture_method: transcript): parse the Claude Code transcript JSONL, attribute each subagent's messages via isSidechain + session/uuid subtree, sum the four usage fields. Proves SC-002 can be an exact interactive measurement without waiting for the Agent SDK (M6).

nodes.label: graphify CLI query interface (explain / query / path)
nodes.norm_label: graphify cli query interface (explain / query / path)

nodes.label: Graph is the source of truth — never invent edges
nodes.norm_label: graph is the source of truth — never invent edges

nodes.label: How it works — the graphify bridge
nodes.norm_label: how it works — the graphify bridge

nodes.label: Workflow — one feature, end to end
nodes.norm_label: workflow — one feature, end to end

nodes.label: platform/ (manager + GUI + orchestrator, empty until M5)
nodes.norm_label: platform/ (manager + gui + orchestrator, empty until m5)

nodes.label: D40 — an agent is assembled, not stored
nodes.norm_label: d40 — an agent is assembled, not stored

nodes.label: The fixture is the encoding of the contracts
nodes.norm_label: the fixture is the encoding of the contracts

nodes.label: R1-S01: Risk register omits misreading failure mode
nodes.norm_label: r1-s01: risk register omits misreading failure mode

nodes.label: R1-S04: Ship a machine-readable conformance checker
nodes.norm_label: r1-s04: ship a machine-readable conformance checker

nodes.label: Tool Grants (union of injected skills, D41)
nodes.norm_label: tool grants (union of injected skills, d41)

nodes.label: Context Hygiene (suggestions.md is the only main-thread read)
nodes.norm_label: context hygiene (suggestions.md is the only main-thread read)

nodes.label: Three-stage council review (opinions → peer review → chairman synthesis)
nodes.rationale: Faithful llm-council method: independent opinions then anonymized peer review is where members catch each other's misses; deleting the peer stage was rejected.
nodes.norm_label: three-stage council review (opinions → peer review → chairman synthesis)

nodes.label: Council human gate
nodes.rationale: The council advises; the human decides — approval is the event that permits token-spending work to begin (D3/D9).

nodes.label: Reduced-grounding flag (FR-019)
nodes.rationale: When no graph.json exists the council degrades to deck-only rather than blocking; a degraded review must never read as fully grounded, so the condition is flagged in suggestions.md and the decision record, visible at the gate.

nodes.label: Reopen interface (--reopen delta|full)
nodes.rationale: v1 ships the manual reopen mechanism (delta = plan diff + triggering finding; full = whole rerun); the automated /speckit-analyze trigger is deferred to analyze integration.

nodes.label: One-round convergence + chairman-only delta check
nodes.rationale: Convergence must be bounded (cost is the M1 risk) yet never ship a known blocking flaw; the cheap chairman delta check after one revision is the cost control that makes single-round convergence safe (D13).

nodes.label: Council spend (SC-002 token metric)
nodes.rationale: council_spend = phase_spend(council) + phase_spend(deck-prep); the first observability datapoint and the number the M1 cost-risk decision is made against, reported alongside the count of capture_method:unavailable records (D47).

nodes.label: Compression boundary (only suggestions.md returns to main thread)
nodes.rationale: The technical spine: the main thread coordinates subagents but never ingests opinion bodies — only the chairman's suggestions.md crosses the session boundary as content.
nodes.norm_label: compression boundary (only suggestions.md returns to main thread)

nodes.label: Status-only returns invariant (S2)
nodes.rationale: Every subagent returns only a path + one-line outcome + trace fragment; all review content is file-mediated, so there is no opinion text in the main thread to leak (makes SC-005's grep sufficient).

nodes.label: Subagent choreography (main-thread orchestrator + leaf subagents)
nodes.rationale: Chosen over one nested council session because Claude Code doesn't reliably nest subagents and nesting hides stage barriers from the resumable artifact tree; the nested form is the M6 SDK realization with an identical invariant.

nodes.label: Member differentiation via lens nudge
nodes.rationale: Five general-purpose members share one base prompt differentiated by soft lenses (correctness, risk, simplicity, testability, sequencing); satisfies D18 'varied prompts' and keeps v1→v2 role critics a template swap, not an architecture change.

nodes.label: Serial orchestrator trace assembly under parallelism
nodes.rationale: 5 parallel members appending to one JSONL would interleave and corrupt lines; the orchestrator appends trace fragments serially after each barrier.
nodes.norm_label: serial orchestrator trace assembly under parallelism

nodes.label: Per-session token capture (Risk R1, OPEN)
nodes.rationale: SC-002 needs real per-session tokens but interactive Claude Code may not expose per-subagent usage; a transcript-capture spike sets capture_method (transcript vs unavailable), exact measurement guaranteed only once the SDK drives sessions (M6).

nodes.label: Graphify query-tool wiring (member receipts-checking)
nodes.rationale: No new tool is built — members call the installed graphify CLI (query/explain/path) directly via Bash to ground critiques in real dependency edges; the differentiator over vanilla llm-council.

nodes.label: council-config.yml (member_count, lenses, model map, max_rounds)
nodes.rationale: Trimming member count (the M1 cost lever) is a one-line config edit, not a code change.

nodes.label: Thin member interface (member-prompt.md {{lens}} slot)
nodes.rationale: A member is anything that receives (deck, plan, tools) and returns a review file, so a multi-provider or role-critic swap is a prompt/config change (FR-003).

nodes.label: Sibling-of-graphify packaging pattern
nodes.rationale: Command skills authored in extensions/*/skills/ and installed to .claude/skills/ so a Spec Kit re-init never clobbers them — the compatibility guarantee graphify documents.

nodes.label: Bootstrap human council (001 Round 1)
nodes.rationale: The council extension does not yet exist, so for its own plan the human is the council (D9); the four suggestions are the human council's, raised directly against plan.md — the decision-record.md contract's first real instance.

nodes.label: Council tasks + execution waves (20 tasks / 8 waves)
nodes.norm_label: council tasks + execution waves (20 tasks / 8 waves)

nodes.label: Feature branch (name = spec ID)
nodes.rationale: The sole phase state that lives outside specs/ — a git ref; the branch and the commit graph ARE the state (D32).

nodes.label: Wave commit (per-wave implement checkpoint)
nodes.rationale: implement is the long, parallel, interruption-prone phase; wave-granular commits make resumability real inside a phase (committed before the [X] mark, R1-S06).

nodes.label: Gate-SHA binding (approval ↔ immutable commit SHA)
nodes.rationale: An approval that floats free of the content it approved is worthless; binding it to an immutable SHA distinguishes 'approved THIS plan' from 'approved A plan that has since changed'.

nodes.label: Stale approval hard-block (FR-009)
nodes.rationale: A recorded approval SHA that no longer matches the artifact does not authorize the dependent phase; detection is working-tree-aware (dirty = stale) and fails closed — a warn-and-override was rejected by name as defeating the binding.

nodes.label: Completion cleanup command (/speckit-git-cleanup)
nodes.rationale: Human-invoked (never automatic — retiring a branch is consequential); integrates trail-preserved, tags, then deletes the branch; a conflict aborts and is surfaced, never auto-resolved.

nodes.label: complete/<spec-id> tag anchor (D52)
nodes.rationale: The immutable completion anchor is the annotated tag (the D19/M5 binding target), enumerable independent of merge topology, so --no-ff is no longer required and ff stays permitted (supersedes D51's merge clause).

nodes.label: Zero-AI runtime / no trace (FR-007)
nodes.rationale: Mechanical git makes no model calls, so it appends no traces.jsonl record of its own — its operations are synchronous shell hooks, not AI sessions (git_ext_spend = 0, satisfied by construction).

nodes.label: No state file — branch/refs are the state (D32)
nodes.rationale: Phase state is inferred from artifacts and refs; gates.yml is a bindings record, not phase state — adding a state file is forbidden.
nodes.norm_label: no state file — branch/refs are the state (d32)

nodes.label: gates.yml (git-ext-owned SHA bindings record)
nodes.rationale: Owner ruling R1-S09/S20: the git ext owns its own artifact rather than co-writing a gate command's section, keeping principle I clean and dissolving the supply/record seam.

nodes.label: Branch birth folded into after_specify (D-R1)
nodes.rationale: By after_specify the spec ID already exists in feature.json so the branch name is read not recomputed (FR-002 guaranteed); a before_specify hook would fire before the ID exists and risk a dir/branch slug mismatch.

nodes.label: Hook architecture (optional:false auto-invoke + stop-on-nonzero)
nodes.rationale: Hard-block/branch/commit hooks are optional:false so the dispatcher auto-invokes them (optional:true is only announced, R1-S01); an explicit stop-on-nonzero clause is a second independent fix (R1-S02). v1 enforcement is prose-level; a mechanical HookExecutor is deferred to M6 (D53).

nodes.label: commit primitive (self-heal branch, scoped staging)
nodes.rationale: One self-healing entry point ensures the branch before committing (kills the silent base-branch failure mode, R1-S12); staging is scoped to specs/NNN + declared outputs, never a repo-wide git add -A that could sweep a stray secret into history (R1-S11).

nodes.label: Installer hook-merge into .specify/extensions.yml
nodes.rationale: The largest, most-failure-prone piece (budgeted own task R1-S15): append-only merge of 7 entries across 3 shapes; verify-gate inserted AHEAD of graphify on shared before_tasks/before_implement so the hard-block runs before graphify's context regen.

nodes.label: Wave-worktree spike → ABANDON (D54)
nodes.rationale: Worktree-per-wave adds cost on disjoint waves and reintroduces the shared-file merge conflict the DAG serialization prevents by construction; the correct isolation layer is the DAG. A null result is a success (D25/D54); resolves I-4.

nodes.label: Taxonomy v0 task categorization (type × specialization)
nodes.rationale: Manual pass (categorize extension ships in M3); devtools-cli-dominant with a security integrity cluster; 0 general (cap 4) → pass; D48 prompt-tag guard checked and inapplicable (zero-AI extension, no LLM-rendered templates).

nodes.label: I-13: graphify emits no nodes for .sh/.yml
nodes.rationale: graphify explain/path return 'No node matching' for shell/YAML, so type could not be graph-derived here and degraded to engineer judgment for 21 of 23 tasks — booked as evidence for the taxonomy v0→v1 review (R1-S22).
nodes.norm_label: i-13: graphify emits no nodes for .sh/.yml

nodes.label: .specify/extensions.yml (shared hook registry)
nodes.rationale: The single graph-grounded shared/mutable collision point; a malformed edit breaks ALL phases, so its merge is orchestrator glue (append-only), never a parallel subagent's job.

nodes.label: Reinstall-survival regression (Finding-1 / I-14)
nodes.rationale: graphify ships speckit-implement-parallel, so the per-wave edit applied only to the installed copy was silently wiped by a graphify reinstall; the regression caught it, fixed by relocating the edit into graphify's source. Booked as a cross-extension seam convention: prefer hook points over source edits (I-14).

nodes.label: M2 dogfood completion (002 build)
nodes.rationale: 14 waves, 23/23 tasks; the M2 exit criterion is met — a full pipeline run on an auto-created branch with phase-tagged commits; 002's only live traces are its council round (council_spend 5,249,858 tok, M1's exit test).

nodes.label: Workforce gate (approved-with-notes, width-4)
nodes.rationale: Width-4 approved (disjoint new-file sets are the strong case of the file-disjointness fallback); spike timebox ≤2h confirmed (D54); bootstrap discipline binding — orchestrator performs commit-before-[X] and gate-freshness by hand until 002's own waves land those mechanisms.

nodes.label: Git-ext command & hook surface (hooks + primitives + cleanup)
nodes.norm_label: git-ext command & hook surface (hooks + primitives + cleanup)

nodes.label: Git-ext tasks + execution waves (23 tasks / 14 waves)
nodes.norm_label: git-ext tasks + execution waves (23 tasks / 14 waves)

nodes.label: Human Gate approval (002, Naren Karthik B M, approved)
nodes.norm_label: human gate approval (002, naren karthik b m, approved)

nodes.label: D52 — complete/<spec-id> tag replaces --no-ff cleanup anchor
nodes.rationale: Owner preamble (ii): a mandatory annotated tag serves the M5 completion-anchor purpose without forcing --no-ff merge topology; ff permitted. Supersedes D51's --no-ff clause only.
nodes.norm_label: d52 — complete/<spec-id> tag replaces --no-ff cleanup anchor

nodes.label: D53 — mechanical HookExecutor enforcement deferred to M6
nodes.rationale: Enforcement is prose-level in v1; no code-level hook enforcement exists in the repo, so tasks must not claim mechanical hook enforcement; drift lint compensates.
nodes.norm_label: d53 — mechanical hookexecutor enforcement deferred to m6

nodes.label: Branch and commit graph ARE the state (D32, no state file)
nodes.rationale: D32 spine: the branch is a ref and gate SHAs live inside the gate artifacts; a state file/DB is forbidden outright, not merely disfavored.
nodes.norm_label: branch and commit graph are the state (d32, no state file)

nodes.label: Branch birth folded into after_specify hook
nodes.rationale: By after_specify the spec ID exists in feature.json, so the branch name is read not re-derived — zero duplication of create-new-feature.sh's NNN+slug logic, FR-002 guaranteed. before_specify was rejected for firing before the ID exists.
nodes.norm_label: branch birth folded into after_specify hook

nodes.label: Command→primitive seam (R1, §F load-bearing integration)
nodes.rationale: Boundaries with no stock hook point (council/triage/gate commits, per-wave implement commits) depend on the calling command invoking the git-ext primitive; a forgotten call leaves that boundary uncommitted.

nodes.label: Gate↔SHA binding: supply/record split
nodes.rationale: Git ext supplies the SHA via sha helper; the gate command that owns its section records <artifact> @ <sha>, so the git ext never becomes a second writer of another phase's artifact (Constitution principle I). This split is what manufactures R1's gate-recording half.

nodes.label: Phase-4 worktree-per-wave spike (FR-015, firewalled)
nodes.rationale: Timeboxed spike giving each parallel wave its own git worktree; writes only to implement.log.md; no FR-001..FR-014 behavior depends on it, deleting it leaves the v1 loop intact.

nodes.label: /speckit-git-cleanup completion command
nodes.rationale: Human-invoked, never automatic because retiring a branch is consequential; integrates the feature and creates the completion anchor.

nodes.label: .specify/extensions.yml shared hook registry
nodes.rationale: The one file with real blast radius — depended on by every /speckit-* phase command; the installer merges append-only, never overwrites; a malformed edit breaks all phases.

nodes.label: Council Round 1 Suggestions — chairman synthesis (002)
nodes.rationale: The compression boundary: the only file under council/ that the main thread and /speckit-council-triage read; opinions stay chairman-only.

nodes.label: optional:true hook-dispatch defect (R1-S01, round headline)
nodes.rationale: Hooks copied graphify's optional:true schema; stock dispatch only announces optional:true hooks and never auto-invokes them, so after_specify branch/commit and before_tasks/before_implement hard-blocks never fire unprompted — the warn-and-override FR-009 rejected on 2026-07-09; falsifies SC-001 and FR-009. Raised on 3 lenses, endorsed by all 5 peers.

nodes.label: Stop-on-nonzero hook clause (R1-S02)
nodes.rationale: Shared dispatch prose says 'wait for it to finish' but never 'abort on failure'; a second independent hard-block failure from S01 — flipping the flag runs the hook but still does not block. Both patches must land or the second is dropped as already covered.

nodes.label: False 'only extensions.yml touched' manifest (R1-S03)
nodes.rationale: Design requires editing five existing files (council-approve SHA-record, implement-parallel per-wave commit, tasks/implement stop-on-nonzero, graphify-context regen); since /speckit-tasks keys off the manifest, R1 could ship with no task.

nodes.label: Cross-extension reinstall-overwrite coupling (R1-S04)
nodes.rationale: R1-seam call sites live in council/graphify-owned skills whose installers rm -rf + cp -R unconditionally on reinstall, silently wiping any edit to the installed copies; resolved by a reinstall-surviving after_council_approve hook.

nodes.label: Working-tree-blind gate SHA check (R1-S05)
nodes.rationale: sha/verify-gate compare committed-HEAD only, so a hand-edited-but-uncommitted approved plan.md false-passes the hard-block — the most direct threat FR-009 exists to catch. Fix: commit-before-read ordering or make verify-gate working-tree-aware.

nodes.label: Wave-commit ordering hole — commit before [X] (R1-S06)
nodes.rationale: implement-parallel marks each task [X] before the per-wave commit, so an interruption cannot distinguish 'wave done, commit missing' from 'wave partial, discard' — breaks principle III and SC-006. Commit before marking [X], or make marker+commit atomic.

nodes.label: priority field is dead code (R1-S07)
nodes.rationale: priority is written as a static 5 and never read; append order lands verify-gate after graphify's heavy context regeneration at the only two real shared keys (before_tasks/before_implement). Installer must insert verify-gate ahead of graphify.

nodes.label: HookExecutor unimplemented — prose-only enforcement (R1-S08)
nodes.rationale: The HookExecutor every skill defers to is referenced 11-13 times and implemented zero times; even a corrected optional:false hook is enforced only by an LLM following prose, undercutting the deck's 'falsifiable without a model call' claim. Deferred to M6 (D53).

nodes.label: GateSHABinding: Human Gate has no @sha field (R1-S09)
nodes.rationale: ## Human Gate fields are reviewer/decision/reviewed; the existing @<sha> citations live in ## Round N / R4, written by a different command at a different time and not satisfying FR-008. Resolved by a git-ext-owned gates.yml binding (S20).
nodes.norm_label: gateshabinding: human gate has no @sha field (r1-s09)

nodes.label: verify-gate fail-closed on unparseable input (R1-S10)
nodes.rationale: An unparseable-but-present gate section that silently passes is a textbook fail-open; require fail-closed plus a format-version guard as an FR-009 acceptance criterion.

nodes.label: commit.sh staging scope ambiguity (R1-S11)
nodes.rationale: 'Stages the feature's changed paths' is ambiguous; every after_* commit fires unattended so a repo-wide git add -A could sweep a stray .env/credential into permanent history. Scope to specs/NNN/** + declared outputs, never repo-wide.

nodes.label: NNN allocation TOCTOU race (R1-S13)
nodes.rationale: Two concurrent /speckit-specify runs race the unlocked directory scan; branch.sh's create-if-absent then converts a loud collision into a silent shared-branch merge. Same-NNN-different-slug is the likelier case; needs locked (flock) allocation + a concurrency test.

nodes.label: Installer extensions.yml hook-merge undercosted (R1-S15)
nodes.rationale: Graphify needed a 193-line embedded-Python YAML merge with a 5-way interpreter fallback to merge 3 uniform entries; git-ext merges 7 entries across 3 shapes into 5 keys + 2 append targets — likely the largest, most failure-prone file, hidden inside '~5 short POSIX sh scripts'.

nodes.label: Stale before_specify assertions (R1-S16)
nodes.rationale: speckit-specify/SKILL.md's closing NOTE and graphify-context.md line 13 both assert the rejected before_specify design, contradicting the ratified after_specify (D51) and pointing a future implementer at the wrong approach.

nodes.label: Descope gate-SHA to record-and-disclose (R1-S21, rejected)
nodes.rationale: Rejected: reproduces the warn-and-override FR-009 rejected by name on 2026-07-09; adopting it would require a spec-delta re-litigating that clarification, and would leave FR-009/SC-004 with nothing to hard-block-test.

nodes.label: complete/<spec-id> tag as M5 anchor (R1-S27 → D52)
nodes.rationale: A lightweight annotated tag serves the same completion-anchor purpose as hard-locking --no-ff without forcing merge topology; adopted as mandatory (beyond member C's optional framing) — D52.
nodes.norm_label: complete/<spec-id> tag as m5 anchor (r1-s27 → d52)

nodes.label: D56 — council_tier standard (cost-controlled ceremony)
nodes.rationale: M3 opts into the cost-controlled council tier, measured at the plan gate against the 5.25M full-tier baseline.

nodes.label: D58 — pipeline order analyze before categorize; determinism tightening
nodes.rationale: analyze runs before categorize because analyze may patch tasks.md (D11), so classification follows stabilization; artifact-layout §2 amended to this order. Also tightens the determinism boundary to a per-skill roster mark (FR-022).
nodes.norm_label: d58 — pipeline order analyze before categorize; determinism tightening

nodes.label: speckit-ext-categorize (the categorizer)
nodes.rationale: A separate Sonnet session that reads tasks.md + plan.md and writes categorization.md only; PROPOSES, never signs off; runtime AI work (unlike 002).

nodes.label: categorization.md (categorizer's sole output)
nodes.rationale: Each task carries (type, specialization, preserves_behavior, tags); single-writer artifact owned by the categorize extension (D37); the categorizer never mutates tasks.md.

nodes.label: agents/assignment.md roster artifact
nodes.rationale: Single-writer artifact owned by the agents extension; carries the ## Workforce Gate roster.

nodes.label: ## Workforce Gate roster & human sign-off
nodes.rationale: The roster plus its human sign-off IS the workforce gate (D9) — the economic guard before implementation spends tokens and the moment network access is approved (D41). approved/approved-with-notes unlocks /speckit-implement-parallel; rejected returns for reassignment.

nodes.label: Blessed taxonomy v0 (8 types × 11 specializations)
nodes.rationale: A closed, blessed enum (D42, taxonomy-v0.md); general is capped, preserves_behavior is a modifier; adding a value is a D-row, not an edit.

nodes.label: Base specialist (curated-static lane)
nodes.rationale: A .claude/agents/<name>.md lane definition selected by (type, specialization); carries a model, no tags; does not evolve (D44).

nodes.label: Skill module (SKILL.md, additive-only library unit)
nodes.rationale: The library's unit of storage; additive-only; carries tags, declared grants, provenance, stats; the only evolving entry (D40, D44).

nodes.label: Assembled agent (base + ≤3 skills, composed at assignment time)
nodes.rationale: Not a stored file: base + ≤3 injected skills, elevated grants = the union of the skills' grants (D40).
nodes.norm_label: assembled agent (base + ≤3 skills, composed at assignment time)

nodes.label: Skill builder (∅-match authorship, the flywheel D24)
nodes.rationale: When a task's tags match no existing skill it authors one additive-only SKILL.md (not a bespoke agent) and persists it with provenance origin:generated; promotion to origin:promoted is a separate manual v1 step. The library's only self-evolving component.
nodes.norm_label: skill builder (∅-match authorship, the flywheel d24)

nodes.label: Assembler/assigner
nodes.rationale: A separate session reading categorization.md + the library, writing assignment.md only; selects one base by (type, specialization), injects ≤3 tag-ranked additive-only skills, logs what the cap trims.

nodes.label: Assembly determinism against a fixed library snapshot (FR-015/SC-005)
nodes.rationale: Determinism is a property of the assignment algorithm, not the LLM skill builder: same categorization.md + same library yields the identical roster. Runs invoking the builder (∅-match) are excluded from the byte-identical claim since LLM authorship is not byte-reproducible and grows the library; SC-005 is measured on gap-free runs.
nodes.norm_label: assembly determinism against a fixed library snapshot (fr-015/sc-005)

nodes.label: Determinism provenance: library vs built marking (FR-022, D58)
nodes.rationale: The roster records per injected skill whether it was library (present at run start) or built (authored this run), making SC-005's gap-free boundary a checkable artifact property — a gap-free run carries zero built-marked skills. Distinct from a skill's lifetime origin.
nodes.norm_label: determinism provenance: library vs built marking (fr-022, d58)

nodes.label: refactor-discipline auto-inject (preserves_behavior true)
nodes.rationale: preserves_behavior:true auto-injects the refactor-discipline skill, counting against the cap of 3 (taxonomy §2.3).

nodes.label: D48 Sonnet-floor code guard (prompt-tagged tasks)
nodes.rationale: A prompt-tagged task must be assembled onto an implementation specialist at the D18 Sonnet floor, enforced as a code-level guard in the assigner — never a docs-exempt non-Sonnet specialist (D48).

nodes.label: Elevated grants = union of injected skills' grants (D41)
nodes.rationale: The assembled agent's elevated-grant set equals the union of its injected skills' declared grants — nothing else grants anything; the roster's mandatory Elevated grants column must never be omitted because approving the roster is approving network/tool access.
nodes.norm_label: elevated grants = union of injected skills' grants (d41)

nodes.label: web_search — system's first elevated grant (OPEN, deferred to plan)
nodes.rationale: Whether the skill-builder role itself declares web_search — the first real network reach in the system (D41) — is posed by the spec, answered by the plan, and decided by the council at plan review; deliberately not a /speckit-clarify target.
nodes.norm_label: web_search — system's first elevated grant (open, deferred to plan)

nodes.label: 20% general cap — categorization fails loudly (FR-004/D42)
nodes.rationale: If count(general) > 0.20 × count(tasks) the categorizer FAILS, writes no categorization.md, and stops the pipeline — the cap is a floor on evidence (D42), a failure not a warning.

nodes.label: Seed library (deferred to the plan)
nodes.rationale: The initial set of base specialists + skills; the plan proposes it, the council reviews it (docs/05 M3's '5-6 specialists' is indicative only).

hyperedges.label: Plan-Defense Council Pipeline
Graphify-Grounded Task & Implementation Flow
Core Spec-Driven Development Pipeline
Three-stage council review pipeline (deck -> members -> chairman -> suggestions)
Gate-to-SHA freshness binding (commit records, sha reads, verify-gate hard-blocks on stale)
Council governance lifecycle (review -> triage -> human gate, threaded by suggestions.md)
Council Review Round (deck to gate)
Agent Assembly at Dispatch (D40)
Gate-Capable Autonomy System (D9)
Three-stage council review pipeline (deck-prep to chairman synthesis)
Council extension governed pipeline (review, triage, human gate)
Council gate to git-ext gate-SHA binding and stale hard-block
Graph-grounded SDD loop (graph → context → tasks → parallel implement)
Optional grounding-hook wiring (extension → hooks → command → skill)
000-sample conformance-fixture artifact tree
M1 Council Extension Build (speckit-ext-council)
000-sample Council Round 1 Deliberation
000-sample Agent Assembly at Workforce Gate
Three-stage council review roles (opinions → peer review → chairman synthesis)
Gate-SHA approval-integrity mechanism
Council end-to-end loop (deck → review → triage → human gate, SC-001)
Five-lens council panel (correctness/risk/simplicity/testability/sequencing)
Convergent optional:true / prose-only hard-block defect (round headline)
Workforce pair pipeline: categorize → assemble → workforce gate
